title: Clear to Engage
brief: MS holds the weapon — but MS holds nothing it can fire on its own. Run the fire sequence: describe the strike task, issue the execution order, and then answer the consent request. The weapon will not release until you give consent. You hold the key.
teaches: The MS power relationship is inverted from FA. FA holds authority over the aircraft; here MS holds the capability but asks MA for consent (MS Vol §1.2.10.10). The fire sequence separates the *what* (MA_TaskMT) from the *when* (MA_TaskCommandMT), then MS sends StrikeConsentRequestMT and waits — it will not fire autonomously. Approve with StrikeConsentRequestStatusMT and the strike completes; ignore it and nothing releases.

objective._doc: No flight. Win = the strike activity reaches COMPLETED. Sequence: MA_TaskMT → MA_TaskStatusMT (CapabilityID assigned) → MA_TaskCommandMT (NEW/execute) → MA_TaskCommandStatusMT ACCEPTED → StrikeConsentRequestMT → StrikeConsentRequestStatusMT (APPROVED) → MA_StrikeActivityMT ENABLED → COMPLETED. holdTicks 2 so the COMPLETED report is delivered before the win lands.
